title: Radiological and nuclear emergencies - Canada.ca
content:
## Radiological and nuclear emergencies
In Canada, every level of government has responsibility in the event of a nuclear or radiological emergency. Response begins at the local or municipal level, and progresses to the provincial and federal levels, depending upon the location, type, and size of the emergency.

## Services and information

### About radiological and nuclear emergencies
Radiological and nuclear emergencies affecting Canadians, International Nuclear Event Scale, classifying a nuclear emergency.

### Previous nuclear incidents and accidents
Fukushima Daichiii, Chernobyl, Three Mile Island, COSMOS 954

### How Canada prepares for radiological and nuclear emergencies
Federal Nuclear Emergency Plan (FNEP), nuclear emergency exercises, nuclear emergency preparedness and response.

### Radiation measurement
Fixed Point Surveillance Network (FPS), Canadian Radiological Monitoring Network (CRMN), Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Monitoring Network, European Radiological Data Exchange Platform (EURDEP).
- Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission
